flashcards.front: What is the function of a pull-up resistor?
flashcards.back: To weakly pull a digital input signal to a high logic level (VDD) when no active device is driving it, preventing a floating state.

flashcards.front: What is the key difference between Push-Pull and Open-Drain output modes?
flashcards.back: Push-Pull can actively drive the signal both high and low. Open-Drain can only actively drive the signal low (to GND) or become high-impedance; it requires an external pull-up resistor to achieve a high state.

flashcards.front: Name the three primary GPIO control registers.
flashcards.back: 1. Data Direction Register (sets input/output). 2. Data Output Register (sets output value/pull-up). 3. Data Input Register (reads pin state).
An input pin that is not connected to a definite voltage source (high or low) and is therefore susceptible to electrical noise, leading to unpredictable readings.
